So Silicon Valley Bank collapses faster than a house of cards in a hurricane, and suddenly Credit Suisse is doing the financial equivalent of checking under the bed for monsters. Because apparently when one bank goes down, the rest start sweating like they all cheated on the same test.

Tech bros who spent years disrupting everything suddenly can't figure out how to open a new bank account. VCs are clutching their checkbooks tighter than their Tesla steering wheels, leaving startups to fund their ping pong tables with loose couch change.

Wine country discovers that 44% of their liquid assets were stored with a bank that had the structural integrity of a paper airplane. Now vineyard owners are probably wishing they'd literally buried their money in wine barrels like pirates.

Federal regulators wake up from their six-year nap and decide maybe banks shouldn't operate like unregulated cryptocurrency exchanges. Shocking development: turns out oversight costs money, who could have predicted this financial revelation.

Community banks start hoarding cash like doomsday preppers stockpile canned beans. Small businesses looking for loans are now getting treated like they're asking to borrow the Hope Diamond for a weekend trip to Vegas.

Food trucks discover their fancy fintech payment systems have more abandonment issues than a reality TV dating show. Because nothing says 'economic stability' like your taco transaction getting declined due to banking sector paranoia.

Are you KIDDING me right now? An EIGHTEEN DOLLAR burrito because some Silicon Valley finance bros couldn't manage risk assessment? I can buy a whole chicken for less than that! This is absolutely unhinged! People are out here charging luxury resort prices for what is essentially beans and rice wrapped in a tortilla because of cascading financial incompetence!
